question: Based on the stacked bar chart showing quarterly restaurant performance indicators, which statement best reflects what can be concluded about overall performance across quarters?

options.A: The chart cannot determine overall performance accurately because it combines unrelated metrics that cannot be meaningfully added together.
options.B: Q1 shows the best overall performance because it has the smallest combined height across all indicators.
options.C: Q1 shows the best overall performance because its indicators remain relatively stable compared with other quarters.
options.D: Q4 shows the best overall performance because it has the highest stacked total.

explanation: The chart stacks unrelated metrics (Sales, Kitchen Temperature, etc.) into a single total. These metrics have different units and meanings (e.g., counts, temperatures, consumption), so adding them together creates a meaningless sum. The stacked height cannot be used to assess overall performance. Only individual metric comparisons are valid.
